name: Haiku (DeepMind JAX Neural Network Library)

performance.notes: JAX JIT compilation; same performance as raw JAX after compilation

agent_readiness.notes: DeepMind's neural network library for JAX. Lifts stateful programming (Sonnet-like) to work with JAX's functional transforms. import haiku as hk. class MyNet(hk.Module): def __call__(self, x): return hk.Linear(10)(x). hk.transform() wraps module into pure init/apply functions. hk.without_apply_rng() for inference. Works with jax.jit, jax.grad, jax.vmap. Less Pythonic than Flax but more familiar for Sonnet users. DeepMind's preferred internal framework for JAX research.

gotchas: Functional programming mindset required (init/apply function pair)
More complex state management than PyTorch modules
Smaller community than Flax or PyTorch Lightning
best_when: JAX-based deep learning research at DeepMind/academic style
Teams familiar with Sonnet (TF) migrating to JAX
Research requiring JAX functional transforms with stateful modules
Reproducing DeepMind papers with reference implementations
avoid_when: Production ML (use PyTorch Lightning or Keras)
When Equinox's simpler API is sufficient
summary: Haiku (dm-haiku) is DeepMind's neural network library for JAX. hk.transform() wraps stateful module classes into pure init/apply function pairs compatible with jax.jit, jax.grad, jax.vmap. Sonnet-inspired API for JAX. More complex than Flax but preferred by DeepMind researchers. Use Flax or Equinox for easier JAX neural network development.
